food_name: Fair Trade Certified Italian Roast Coffee Beans
region_of_origin: Italy (roast style); coffee beans sourced from Fair Trade certified origins
food_subgroup: Roasted coffee beans / ground coffee (dark roast)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301339 covers roasted coffee beans as a category; no specific Italian Roast or Fair Trade variant code exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers roasted coffee generically; no Italian Roast dark-roast sub-code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34725008 is the substance-level code for coffee; no roast-level specificity available.

serving.description: 1 serving brewed coffee (approximately 240 ml prepared from ~10 g ground beans)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single serving is approximately 10 g of ground Italian Roast coffee beans used to brew one 240 ml cup of black coffee.

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, brewed from grounds, prepared with tap water — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14209; supplemented with USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods roasted coffee bean data #2346501
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are reported per 100 g of roasted coffee beans (dry weight basis) using USDA FoodData Central data for roasted coffee beans (FDC #2346501 and SR Legacy #14209). Italian Roast is a dark roast profile of Arabica beans; no material nutrient difference from standard roasted coffee beans is established in the literature. Values represent the dry roasted bean, not the brewed beverage. Energy and macros for roasted coffee beans are well-characterised in USDA data.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of coffee and coffee-based beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; Augustin et al. (2002) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.source_match_notes: Black coffee and roasted coffee beans contain negligible glycemic carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Augustin et al.) confirm that black coffee has an effectively zero glycemic response. Per the Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) to satisfy numeric requirements; gi_category is low; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as the serving contains no glycemic carbohydrates.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (6 mg per 100 g); caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Italian Roast coffee beans are very low in sodium and do not contribute meaningfully to dietary sodium load; however, individuals with hypertension should be aware that caffeine in brewed coffee can cause a transient, dose-dependent rise in blood pressure, and moderate consumption (up to 3–4 cups per day) is generally considered acceptable for most hypertensive patients per clinical guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible glycemic carbohydrates; chlorogenic acids may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dark roast coffee beans contain negligible glycemic carbohydrates and have a glycemic index effectively at zero, making brewed black Italian Roast coffee a low-glycemic beverage suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; epidemiological evidence suggests habitual unsweetened coffee consumption is associated with reduced risk of Type 2 Diabetes, potentially mediated by chlorogenic acids and other polyphenols.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Diterpenes (cafestol, kahweol) in unfiltered preparations; antioxidant polyphenols in filtered coffee
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: When brewed using a paper filter, Italian Roast coffee removes most cafestol and kahweol diterpenes that can raise LDL cholesterol; filtered dark roast coffee consumed in moderation (3–5 cups per day) is associated with neutral to beneficial cardiovascular outcomes in prospective cohort studies, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should prefer filtered brewing methods and avoid adding cream or sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fair Trade Certified Italian Roast is a dark roast whole-bean or ground coffee product sold by Starbucks in Singapore, available in 250 g retail packages. Italian Roast represents one of the darkest roast profiles in Starbucks' lineup, characterised by an intense, rich, and deep flavour with prominent notes of caramelised sugar and a smoky, bittersweet finish. The beans are sourced from Fair Trade certified farms, ensuring ethical sourcing standards and fair compensation for farmers. The roasting process drives off most moisture and volatile acids, producing beans with a dark, oily surface and a bold, low-acidity cup profile. When brewed as black coffee, Italian Roast contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates and is effectively calorie-free as a beverage, making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose or caloric intake. As dry roasted beans, the product is nutritionally dense per 100 g, providing protein (~14 g), fat (~15 g), and significant dietary fibre (~34 g), along with high potassium (~1970 mg), niacin (~17 mg), and iron (~4 mg). The product is available in whole bean or custom-ground formats at Starbucks Singapore locations. It is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Clinically, unsweetened dark roast coffee is associated with reduced risk of Type 2 Diabetes and neutral-to-beneficial cardiovascular outcomes when consumed in moderation via filtered brewing methods.
common_user_queries: Is dark roast coffee good for diabetics?
How many calories in Starbucks Italian Roast?
What does Italian Roast taste like?
Is Italian Roast coffee low GI?
Can I drink dark roast coffee if I have hypertension?
What is the difference between Italian Roast and French Roast?
How much caffeine in Italian Roast?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from USDA FoodData Central data for roasted coffee beans (SR Legacy and Foundation Foods), which is an approved primary source. Italian Roast is a standard dark roast Arabica profile with no material nutrient deviation from generic roasted coffee beans established in the literature. The product-specific 250 g retail pack from Starbucks Singapore does not have an independent nutritional panel in an approved database; therefore confidence is medium rather than high. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database data for black coffee, which is an approved GI source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are reported per 100 g of dry roasted coffee beans, not per 100 ml of brewed beverage. The product is sold as a 250 g retail pack of whole beans or ground coffee. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for roasted coffee beans; naturally zero by product composition. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as coffee beans contain no cholesterol. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; roasting destroys residual ascorbic acid. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for roasted coffee. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for roasted coffee beans. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero; plant-derived food contains no B12. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for roasted coffee. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero; plant-derived food contains no vitamin D. 10. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for roasted coffee beans. 11. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as a standard brewed serving from 10 g beans contains no glycemic carbohydrates. 12. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg are all sourced from USDA FoodData Central FDC #2346501 and SR Legacy #14209. 13. This product is sold at Starbucks Singapore (field survey listing: Starbucks - One Fullerton) at SGD 17.70 for 250 g. 14. Custom grind options are available in-store. Items carry a minimum 14-day best-before date. 15. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Beverages' as the product is a coffee bean/ground coffee product intended for beverage preparation.
